examples.text: وَرَأَيْتَ ٱلنَّاسَ يَدْخُلُونَ فِي دِينِ ٱللّٰهِ أَفْوَاجًا
examples.english: And you see the people entering into the religion of Allah in multitudes

senses.gloss: cohort, battalion, regiment (a military unit of size varying by state)

etymology.text:
Etymology tree
Middle Persian pdk'bor.
Arabic فَيْج (fayj)
Arabic فَوْج
From earlier فَيْج (fayj, “legman, courier; company”), borrowed from Middle Persian [Book Pahlavi needed] (pdk' /⁠payg⁠/, “foot-soldier, foot-walker, courier”) (Classical Persian پیگ (payg)).
